name: libfile-which-perl
title: libfile-which-perl
summary: Perl module for searching paths for executable programs
description:
Perl module for searching paths for executable programs
File::Which simulates the behavior of the standard 'which' program: it
searches directories of a user's PATH (as returned by File::Spec)
looking for executable files matching a given name.
.
On Debian /usr/bin/which is guaranteed to be available, but going
through the Perl implementation can still save a fork.
use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use libfile-which-perl because Perl module for searching paths for executable programs.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use libfile-which-perl as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair libfile-which-perl with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `libfile-which-perl --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
